records.ety: [OE. triacle a sovereign remedy, theriac, OF. triacle, F. thriaque (cf. Pr. triacla, tiriaca, Sp. & It. triaca, teriaca), L. theriaca an antidote against the bite of poisonous animals, Gr. <?/, fr. <?/ of wild or venomous beasts, fr. qhri`on a beast, a wild beast, dim. of qh`r a beast. Cf. Theriac.]

defs: A remedy against poison. See Theriac, 1.
quotes: We kill the viper, and make treacle of him.
Christ which is to every harm treacle.

defs: Molasses; sometimes, specifically, the molasses which drains from the sugar-refining molds, and which is also called sugarhouse molasses.
notes: ☞ In the United States molasses is the common name; in England, treacle.

defs: A saccharine fluid, consisting of the inspissated juices or decoctions of certain vegetables, as the sap of the birch, sycamore, and the like.
